user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a loading skeleton while Horizons data is fetched so that the page layout is stable and I'm not staring at a blank screen.
expected_behaviour: On mount, `useHorizonsQuery` fires `GET /api/v1/dashboard/horizons/` (staleTime 60s, only when `isLoggedIn()`). While `isLoading` is true the page renders `HorizonsSkeleton`: an `animate-pulse` set of 3 glass-card placeholder blocks with stub title bars and varying-width content lines. The skeleton disappears as soon as data resolves. Unauthenticated visitors are redirected to `/login` by the AppShell before the page renders.
inventory_notes: Query is disabled unless `isLoggedIn()` returns truthy. AppShell redirects unauthenticated users before reaching this page; `/horizons` is not in `publicPages`.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see an error message if Horizons data fails to load so that I know the system is unavailable rather than thinking the page is empty.
expected_behaviour: If `useHorizonsQuery` returns a non-null `error`, the page renders only the 'Horizons' heading and a `glass-card-horizons` block with `text-rose-text` reading 'Failed to load Horizons.' followed by `error.message` (if the error is an `Error` instance) or 'Please try again.' There is no retry button; the user must refresh the page.
inventory_notes: No retry affordance in the UI — relies on the user doing a full page refresh or waiting for TanStack Query's default retry logic.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see my last 30 days of engagement as a colour-coded grid so that I can quickly see where I've been active and where I've dropped off.
expected_behaviour: The `MomentumStrip` renders a responsive grid (10 cols on mobile, 15 on md, 30 on lg) of square cells — one per day — for the 30 days in `data.momentum`. Each cell is coloured bright signal (with glow) if in the current streak, dim signal (45% opacity) if active but not in streak, or low-opacity surface-elevated if inactive. A dot is overlaid on cells where `has_journal` is true and the day is active. Each cell has a `title` and `aria-label` describing date, message count, journal presence, and streak membership. The whole component has `role='img'` with a top-level aria-label summarising streak length. The streak badge above the grid shows 'N DAY STREAK' (signal styling) when streak > 0, 'No active streak' when there is some history but no current streak, or 'Start a conversation to build momentum' when there is no activity at all. A legend below keys colour to state.
inventory_notes: Streak calculation is client-side: iterates `days` right-to-left, breaking on the first inactive day. No click/interaction on individual cells — display only. Grid column count is driven by Tailwind classes `grid-cols-10 md:grid-cols-15 lg:grid-cols-30`; the 15 and 30 breakpoints require those classes to be in the Tailwind safelist.

feature: Weekly Pulse — scrollable week rows with expand
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to browse my recent weekly summaries and expand any week with a written report so that I can reflect on how past weeks went.
expected_behaviour: The `WeeklyPulse` component receives two arrays from the API response: `weekly_pulse` (assistant-synthesised pulse rows with a `WeekRating` and `top_win` string) and `weekly_documents` (journal documents keyed to a week). When `weekly_pulse.length > 0` it renders entries from that array; otherwise falls back to `weekly_documents`. If both are empty, it shows 'Your weekly reflections will appear here. The first one arrives this Monday.' Each week renders as a `WeekRow` with ISO week number badge (e.g. 'W24'), date range string, and a rating emoji (🚀 thumbs-up, 🧘 meh, 🔥 thumbs-down) or none if no rating. Older rows are progressively faded (opacity steps down by 0.12 per index, floored at 0.55). Rows with non-empty `markdown` (documents only) show a chevron and can be clicked: toggling `expanded` state in `useState`. When expanded, markdown is rendered via `MarkdownRenderer` inside a surface-elevated container. The button is `aria-expanded` tracked; rows without markdown disable the button and hide the chevron.
inventory_notes: Rating emoji mapping is fixed client-side: thumbs-up → 🚀, meh → 🧘, thumbs-down → 🔥. Expand state is local per WeekRow; no server interaction. `isoWeekNumber` is computed entirely client-side from the `week_start` ISO date string.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see all my active goals in a card grid so that I can quickly review what I'm working towards and navigate into the journal for any goal.
expected_behaviour: After data loads, `data.goals` is filtered client-side: any goal whose `preview` contains the placeholder strings '[Goal Name]' or '[Specific, measurable outcome]' is excluded as a template artifact. Remaining `realGoals` are rendered in a 1-col (md: 2-col) `GoalCard` grid inside the 'Active Goals' `HorizonsSection`. If no real goals remain, the section shows 'No goals yet. Write about your goals in your journal, and your assistant will help you track them.' Each `GoalCard` shows the goal title (extracted from `preview`'s first `###` heading if the stored title is 'goals'), a stripped-markdown preview (max 3 lines), and the `updated_at` date in short locale format. Clicking the card navigates to `/journal/goal/<slug>` (Next.js `<Link>`). On hover, an 'View in Journal →' label becomes visible (hidden on sm, opacity transition on sm+).
inventory_notes: Template-goal filtering (`[Goal Name]`, `[Specific, measurable outcome]`) happens on the frontend. `extractDisplayTitle` falls back through stored title → first `###` heading → 'Untitled Goal'. `extractGoalPreview` strips markdown structural lines and metadata bullet points before calling `stripMarkdown`.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to confirm that an assistant observation is accurate so that my assistant keeps that pattern in its model of me.
expected_behaviour: Each open `HorizonsAssistantInsight` is rendered as an `InsightCard` under the 'What I remember' section (only shown when `data.assistant_insights.length > 0`). The card shows pillar (and `topic_display_name` if present), the `statement` text, a 'New observation' (sky) badge, and the `created_at` date. Clicking 'Looks right' button immediately sets local `optimisticStatus` to `'confirmed'` via `useState`, changing the card's left-border and badge to emerald ('Confirmed') and hiding the action buttons. The `useConfirmInsightMutation` fires `POST /api/v1/insights/insights/<id>/confirm/` with no body. On success the ['horizons'] query is invalidated and the card shows 'Confirmed <date>' in the footer. On API error, `optimisticStatus` is reset to null and the buttons reappear. Both buttons are disabled (`disabled={isPending}`) while any mutation is in-flight.
inventory_notes: Optimistic update is local state only — no TanStack Query cache write. On error the optimistic state is cleared. The API also accepts an optional `note` string body, but the UI does not expose a note input field — it always calls with no body.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to mark an assistant observation as incorrect so that my assistant corrects its model and stops acting on a false assumption.
expected_behaviour: Clicking 'Not quite' on an open `InsightCard` immediately sets local `optimisticStatus` to `'refuted'`, flipping the border and badge to rose ('Refuted') and hiding action buttons. `useRefuteInsightMutation` fires `POST /api/v1/insights/insights/<id>/refute/` with no body. On success the ['horizons'] query is invalidated. On API error, `optimisticStatus` is reset to null. Both buttons are disabled while any mutation is in-flight. Refuted cards display no confirm-date in the footer (the code guards `status === 'confirmed' && insight.last_confirmed_at`).
inventory_notes: Same optimistic/rollback pattern as confirm. API accepts optional `note` body but the UI never sends it. Expired insights (status='expired') also render with a grey badge and no action buttons, following the same `isResolved` guard.

feature: Topics I've learned — voice register meta-state display
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see which topics my assistant has learned about and what communication style adjustments I've requested so that I understand why my assistant talks the way it does.
expected_behaviour: `TopicSignalsSection` receives `data.topic_signals` (may be `null`/empty). If the array is empty or null the section renders nothing at all. For each `HorizonsTopicSignal` a `TopicRow` is rendered showing: the `topic_display_name` heading, the `pillar` in monospace uppercase, a data summary ('Nw of data · confirmed N, corrected N' or 'no data yet · nothing confirmed yet'). If `has_goal` is true a 'Linked goal' pill (signal colours) is shown. If `register_offset !== 0` an `OverrideBadge` pill is rendered showing either '+1 on this topic/pillar — you asked me to be more direct' or '-1 on this topic/pillar — you asked me to ease off', using `register_scope` to say 'topic' or 'pillar'. Section subtitle reads 'The meta-state behind my voice. To shift it, tell me — e.g. 'just be direct about dining' or 'ease off subscriptions'.' This section is read-only; there are no interactive controls.
inventory_notes: The voice-register adjustment itself is made by talking to the assistant in Telegram/LINE, not via this UI. This section is purely a read-only audit trail of what the assistant has learned.

feature: Pending goal extraction — accept as goal or task
user_story: As a subscriber, I want to approve AI-extracted goals or tasks from my journal so that they are formally added to my tracked goals or task list.
expected_behaviour: The 'Suggestions from your journal' section is only rendered when `data.pending_extractions.length > 0`. Each `HorizonsPendingExtraction` renders a `PendingGoal` card with either an 'AI Extraction' badge (accent colours, for kind='goal') or 'Pattern Found' badge (signal colours, for kind='task'), showing the extracted quote, confidence level, and source date. Clicking 'Accept Goal' or 'Accept Task' fires `useApproveExtractionMutation` → `POST /api/v1/journal/extractions/<id>/approve/`. While pending the button text changes to 'Saving...' and both buttons are disabled. On success, local `resolved` state is set to 'approved' and the card body is replaced with 'Added to your goals!' or 'Added to your tasks!' (signal green). The ['horizons'] query is also invalidated. Clicking 'Dismiss' fires `useDismissExtractionMutation` → `POST /api/v1/journal/extractions/<id>/dismiss/`. On success, `resolved` is set to 'dismissed' and the card unmounts entirely (returns null).
inventory_notes: Both mutations invalidate the ['horizons'] query, but the approved/dismissed state is also reflected immediately via local component state so there is no flash between cache invalidation and re-render. There is no undo. Error states from the mutations are not shown to the user — the card stays in its action state silently on failure.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want to see a segmented bar representing AI model capability level so that I understand the intelligence tier of the model serving me.
expected_behaviour: `IntelligenceMeter` is a pure display component (no API call of its own). It accepts a `level` (either a single number 1–10 or a `[lo, hi]` tuple), an optional `label`, and a `compact` boolean. In full mode it renders a labelled 10-bar segmented meter with bars coloured: emerald (fill ≥ 9), sky (≥ 7), amber (≥ 5), rose (< 5). Unfilled bars are `bg-border-strong`. Bars are `flex-1 h-1.5 rounded-sm`. In compact mode it renders a single-line inline row of 10 thin bars (`h-2.5 w-1`) beside the display string (e.g. '6-7/10'). The range tuple displays 'lo-hi/10'; a single number displays 'N/10'. The exported `TIER_INTELLIGENCE` map currently maps only 'starter' → [6, 7].
inventory_notes: No data-fetch dependency — purely a presentational utility. Used wherever a tier or model capability level needs to be visualised. The only current tier mapping is 'starter': [6, 7].

feature: Gravity insights strip — cross-surface open insights teaser
user_story: As a subscriber viewing the Gravity (finance) tab, I want to see a preview of the most recent open Gravity-pillar assistant observations so that I know there are things to review without having to visit Horizons first.
expected_behaviour: `GravityInsightsStrip` re-uses the same `useHorizonsQuery` cache (no extra fetch). It filters `data.assistant_insights` to those where `pillar === 'gravity'` and `status === 'open'`, taking the first 3. If none exist the component returns null and renders nothing. Otherwise it renders a `glass-card-horizons` strip with a 'Your assistant noticed' header, a count label ('N new observation(s) waiting for your confirmation'), a bulleted list of up to 3 insight statements, and a 'View in Horizons →' link (`href='/horizons'`). On desktop (sm+) the link is in the header row; on mobile a second link renders below the list. There are no confirm/dismiss actions here — the strip is read-only.
inventory_notes: Shares the TanStack Query 'horizons' cache with the Horizons page so no duplicate fetch occurs if both are active. Rendered on the Gravity/finance tab, not on /horizons itself. Mobile vs desktop link duplication is purely cosmetic with `sm:hidden`/`sm:inline-flex` classes.

user_story: As a subscriber, I want sections of the Horizons page to fade/slide in sequentially so that the page feels alive and draws my eye across the content.
expected_behaviour: Each `HorizonsSection` wrapper (and the 'Suggestions from your journal' div) has the `animate-reveal` CSS class and a `style={{ animationDelay: '<N>ms' }}` prop. Delays are staggered: Momentum at 100ms, Weekly Pulse at 200ms, Active Goals at 350ms, What I Remember at 425ms, Topics I've Learned at 450ms, Suggestions at 500ms. The animation behaviour is defined by the `animate-reveal` Tailwind/CSS class in `globals.css`. No user interaction; purely cosmetic.
inventory_notes: No `prefers-reduced-motion` guard is applied in the component code — this is a potential a11y gap. The `animate-reveal` class itself may respect reduced-motion if defined that way in globals.css.
